titlestmt.author: Compiled by Jonathan Manton and Anne Rhodes

controlnote.p: This collection contains 96 published digital objects.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday November 10, 2025 at 20:00
Collection title updated. Original collection title was New Music USA interviews. Now changed to NewMusicBox Interviews
Material from 2023 additions added to finding aid, with some exceptions based on availability and restrictions.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Approximately 300 interviews as of April 2025. Interviews conducted for NewMusicBox, the online magazine for New Music USA. Some interviews are restricted or not yet available for streaming.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. The finding aid includes links to stream most interviews online via Oral History of American Music's (OHAM) AV access system, Aviary. Yale users can login to Aviary using their Yale University ID, to stream these interviews without restriction. Other users are able to request access to interviews directly through Aviary. For access instructions, and to request transcripts of an interview, see OHAM's website.
Some interviews may be restricted. Access to these restricted interviews may only be granted to a patron who has first obtained direct permission from the interviewee or their estate.
Some interviews may not have been digitized or are otherwise not available to stream online.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for Oral History of American Music materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast Oral History of American Music materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.
For this collection, rights for each interview are jointly owned by New Music USA and the interviewee in each instance.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: NMUSA, NewMusicBox Interviews, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee and/or title of ensemble, organization, or event.

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p: When processing the computer files in this collection, a number of non-essenital system generated files were removed, leaving just the audio and video files.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Some computer files in this collection incorporate video (.mov and .mp4) and audio content (.ogg and .wav). Streaming versions of each digital video file have been provided for access.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The computer files in this collection were transfered to Yale via File Transfer Protocol. Where appropraite, .mp4 surrogates of each video file were created as access copies, to enable online streaming.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Includes both audio (.ogg) and video (.mp4) copies of the interview.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: Includes both audio (.wav) and video (.mov) copies of the interview.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "First Words: A Panel Discussion About Reviewing Premier," part of the 2002 conference of the Music Critics Association of North America (MCANA) in Santa Fe, NM.

odd.head: Additional Information
odd.p: Label on container indicates this is an interview with Marin Alsop. However, the label on the Mini DV tape itself, indicates this is an interview with Jerod Tate, as well as some rehearsal footage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May be second part to item dated 2011-2012
